results.title: Remove owner calls can be replayed to remove a different owner at the same index, leading to severe issues when combined with lack of last owner guard
results.reasoning: The described issue is that removeOwnerAtIndex can be replayed cross-chain via executeWithoutChainIdValidation, and because owner indices may differ across chains, the same index can remove a different owner on each chain. The proposed fix is to include the owner value with the index (or remove by owner) to ensure the intended owner is removed. In the audit content, items #11 and #13 do discuss cross-chain replay of owner management and replayed removal causing bricking (and #1 discusses missing last-owner guard), but none identify the specific index-mismatch root cause nor the fix of binding the index to the expected owner. They address selective replay/order and last-owner removal, not the “same index removes different owner across chains” flaw. Therefore, the specific vulnerability was not detected.
